scenario_results.reason: The lane advanced normal pickup fulfillment only when OMS, POS, customer, inventory, item-master, substitution, staged tote, cold-chain, and handoff evidence aligned.
Missing store, jurisdiction, product identity, cold-chain, substitution, and inventory prerequisites produced a blocked-state exception note instead of assumed execution.
The lane refused completion without staged tote, pickup, handoff, service-desk, and unresolved-item closure evidence.
Receiving, inventory, item-master, scale, and lot-code conflicts were held for rework and routed to the proper owner before allocation proceeded.
Refund, service-credit, and rain-check exceptions outside delegated authority were routed to guest services or the refund owner with controlling evidence.
Recall and stop-sale boundaries blocked sale, pickup, substitution, and completion for affected items and escalated to food safety and operations owners.
Order, payment, delivery, CRM, and service-ticket mismatches blocked closure and routed to payment or guest-services owners.
High-volume storm execution retained order, SKU, UPC, PLU, inventory, service, safety, blocker, rework, and completion traceability.
The lane preserved realistic grocery operations terminology and artifact shapes rather than falling back to generic retail support language.
Fraud-sensitive repeated refund and substitution claims preserved evidence and escalated to loss prevention instead of being adjudicated in-lane.
Conflicting public, tenant, franchise, jurisdictional, or program guidance was recorded as a precedence conflict requiring escalation.
Tenant-specific substitution, refund, SNAP/EBT, cold-chain, and recall-pull controls were applied without inventing authority or weakening the public baseline.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.90 threshold with complete ability coverage for the execution-processing-and-fulfillment and retail-consumer-and-hospitality contract.
The package stays inside grocery operations execution and does not claim pricing, refund, food-safety, fraud, payment, legal, or executive exception authority outside the lane.
